concerns.text: The rule `|reported_p - reconstructed_p| > 0.05` treats p‑values as interchangeable quantities; p‑values are not linear measures of evidence. This arbitrary absolute‑difference threshold threatens construct validity of the ‘inconsistent’ label.

concerns.location: plan.md:Inconsistency Detection (FR‑004) – effect size relative difference > 5 %
concerns.text: Using a fixed 5 % relative‑difference cutoff for effect sizes is not justified by the domain literature and may flag harmless rounding differences as inconsistencies, undermining construct validity.

Power analysis is performed only for the overall binomial prevalence test. Subgroup analyses (domain/year, FR‑032) are not powered; the plan does not address the sample size needed to detect differences within those sub‑populations.

The synthetic dataset is generated using the same statistical models as the reconstruction step, so it cannot capture real‑world extraction errors or reporting quirks. Precision/recall measured on this set may overstate performance on actual web‑scraped summaries.

The plan merely flags or subsamples domains that exceed 30 % but does not specify a principled method for subsampling or for propagating the resulting uncertainty into the prevalence estimate, leaving a potential confound uncontrolled.

concerns.location: plan.md:Baseline Handling (FR‑012) – average of variant rates
concerns.text: When a baseline conversion rate is missing, reconstructing the absolute effect size by averaging the two variant rates can bias the effect‑size estimate, especially for asymmetric variants, which may inflate or deflate inconsistency flags.

Success Criterion SC‑005 (parsing‑error rate ≤ 5 % of total summaries) is absent from the plan. A dedicated validation step or monitoring metric should be added to ensure this error‑rate threshold is checked.

Success Criterion SC‑026 (Monte‑Carlo validation passes for all statistical tests with absolute difference ≤ 0.01) is not mentioned. The plan should explicitly state that each statistical test implementation will be verified against Monte‑Carlo estimates meeting SC‑026.

The plan describes the extraction phase (Phase 3) but does not explicitly state that the extracted `ABSummary` records are validated against the `extracted_summary.schema.yaml` contract. Adding a step or test that validates each `ABSummary` against its schema would close the contract‑exercise gap.

The synthetic validation set is generated using the same statistical models as the reconstruction step and then the full pipeline is run on it without any mention of deliberately injected inconsistencies. This makes the evaluation of the inconsistency‑detection component (precision/recall/F1) potentially trivial (always perfect) and not independent of the implementation, undermining the ability to empirically assess detection performance.

The rule `|reported_p - reconstructed_p| > 0.05` treats p‑values as interchangeable quantities; p‑values are not linear measures of evidence. This arbitrary absolute‑difference threshold threatens construct validity of the ‘inconsistent’ label.

concerns.location: plan.md:Inconsistency Detection (FR‑004) – effect size relative difference > 5 %
concerns.text: Using a fixed 5 % relative‑difference cutoff for effect sizes is not justified by the domain literature and may flag harmless rounding differences as inconsistencies, undermining construct validity.

Power analysis is performed only for the overall binomial prevalence test. Subgroup analyses (domain/year, FR‑032) are not powered; the plan does not address the sample size needed to detect differences within those sub‑populations.

The synthetic dataset is generated using the same statistical models as the reconstruction step, so it cannot capture real‑world extraction errors or reporting quirks. Precision/recall measured on this set may overstate performance on actual web‑scraped summaries.

The plan merely flags or subsamples domains that exceed 30 % but does not specify a principled method for subsampling or for propagating the resulting uncertainty into the prevalence estimate, leaving a potential confound uncontrolled.

concerns.location: plan.md:Baseline Handling (FR‑012) – average of variant rates
concerns.text: When a baseline conversion rate is missing, reconstructing the absolute effect size by averaging the two variant rates can bias the effect‑size estimate, especially for asymmetric variants, which may inflate or deflate inconsistency flags.

The secondary consistency score uses a hard threshold `|log_p_reported - log_p_recon| > 0.3` (≈ factor 2) without empirical or literature justification; this arbitrary cutoff may misclassify summaries and should be supported by validation or sensitivity analysis.

Success Criterion SC‑005 (parsing‑error rate ≤ 5 % of total summaries) is absent from the plan. A dedicated validation step or monitoring metric should be added to ensure this error‑rate threshold is checked.

Success Criterion SC‑026 (Monte‑Carlo validation passes for all statistical tests with absolute difference ≤ 0.01) is not mentioned. The plan should explicitly state that each statistical test implementation will be verified against Monte‑Carlo estimates meeting SC‑026.

The plan describes the extraction phase (Phase 3) but does not explicitly state that the extracted `ABSummary` records are validated against the `extracted_summary.schema.yaml` contract. Adding a step or test that validates each `ABSummary` against its schema would close the contract‑exercise gap.

The synthetic validation set is generated using the same statistical models as the reconstruction step and then the full pipeline is run on it without any mention of deliberately injected inconsistencies. This makes the evaluation of the inconsistency‑detection component (precision/recall/F1) potentially trivial (always perfect) and not independent of the implementation, undermining the ability to empirically assess detection performance.

new_concerns.text: The secondary consistency score uses a hard threshold `|log_p_reported - log_p_recon| > 0.3` (≈ factor 2) without empirical or literature justification; this arbitrary cutoff may misclassify summaries and should be supported by validation or sensitivity analysis.

concerns.text: Tasks T076 (compute checksums) and T077 (extend manifest.json) are both marked `[P]` implying parallel execution, yet T077 depends on the output of T076. The `[P]` tag should be removed or changed to reflect the required sequential ordering (T076 → T077).

responses.response: Added a justification for the absolute p‑value difference threshold and introduced a complementary relative‑log‑scale check to ensure statistical rigor while still satisfying FR‑004.
responses.what_changed: Inserted a "Threshold Justification" subsection in plan.md and expanded the inconsistency detection description in research.md.

responses.response: Provided a literature‑based rationale for the 5 % relative effect‑size cutoff and added an alternative absolute‑difference rule for very small effects.
responses.what_changed: Added an "Effect Size Threshold Justification" subsection in plan.md and clarified the rule in research.md.

responses.response: Extended the power‑analysis component to cover subgroup (domain/year) prevalence tests, computing a minimum per‑subgroup size and issuing a warning if a subgroup is under‑powered.
responses.what_changed: Added a "Subgroup Power Analysis" paragraph to research.md and a corresponding validation step in plan.md.

responses.response: Enhanced synthetic data generation to inject realistic reporting quirks (rounding, missing fields, inequality p‑values) and to deliberately introduce a controlled proportion of inconsistent records.
responses.what_changed: Added a "Synthetic Validation Set with Injected Inconsistencies" section to research.md and a new pipeline step in plan.md.

responses.response: Specified a stratified subsampling algorithm to cap any domain at 30 % and introduced a bootstrap procedure to propagate uncertainty into the bias‑adjusted prevalence estimate.
responses.what_changed: Added a "Bias‑Adjustment Methodology" subsection in plan.md and detailed the bootstrap approach in research.md.

responses.response: Replaced the simple average baseline with a sample‑size‑weighted average and documented the fallback to external baseline estimates when both variant rates are missing.
responses.what_changed: Added a "Baseline Handling Alternative" paragraph in plan.md and reflected it in research.md.

responses.response: Inserted an explicit monitoring step for parsing‑error rate (SC‑005) and added it to the Phase Map.
responses.what_changed: Added a "Parsing‑Error Rate Monitoring" task in plan.md and referenced SC‑005 in research.md.

responses.response: Explicitly listed Monte‑Carlo validation of all statistical tests as a required pipeline phase, tying it to SC‑026.
responses.what_changed: Added a "Monte‑Carlo Validation" step to the Phase Map in plan.md and described the validation criteria in research.md.

responses.response: Added a schema‑validation step after extraction to ensure each `ABSummary` conforms to `extracted_summary.schema.yaml` before further processing.
responses.what_changed: Inserted a "Schema Validation" task into the Phase Map of plan.md and referenced it in research.md.

responses.response: Ensured the synthetic validation set includes a known proportion of deliberately inconsistent records and varied reporting styles, enabling a non‑trivial assessment of precision/recall.
responses.what_changed: Expanded the synthetic‑data description in research.md and added a generation‑parameter step in plan.md.

new_concerns.text: Success Criterion SC‑001 (extraction accuracy ≥ 95 % on a manually annotated validation set) is not referenced anywhere in the implementation plan. The plan should include a dedicated validation step or metric that measures extraction accuracy against the annotated set and ties it to SC‑001.

Tasks T076 (compute checksums) and T077 (extend manifest.json) are both marked `[P]` implying parallel execution, yet T077 depends on the output of T076. The `[P]` tag should be removed or changed to reflect the required sequential ordering (T076 → T077).
